content.mi:
Te Kaitiaki o te Moana

I te wā i wehe atu ai a Te Tahi-o-Te-Rā i tēnei ao, ka hoki tōna wairua ki te moana.

I reira ka huri ia hei taniwha—

hei marakihau,

hei kaitiaki mō ngā wai hōhonu.

Kei Ōtara tōna kāinga, kei roto i tana ana i raro i te wai, ā, ka huri haere ia i te whānui o Moana-a-Toi.
content.en:
Becoming the Guardian of the Sea

When Te Tahi-o-Te-Rā eventually left this world, his wairua returned to the ocean.

There he transformed into a taniwha—

a marakihau,

a guardian of the deep waters.

His home is in Ōtara, in his underwater cave, and he travels the vast Moana-a-Toi.
